boundary: A -> B only; raw A remains preserved provenance; B contains bounded summaries, source references, rules, tests, and calibration targets; C is deferred.

sources.role: pre-vessel probe findings and calibration implications
headings: Key Finding 1: Retrieval Quality Matters A Lot
Key Finding 2: No-Citation Behavior Is Usually Bounded But Less Precise
Key Finding 3: Provenance Correction Works With Correct Evidence
Key Finding 4: Gate Wording Still Needs Care
Key Finding 5: Signal vs. Noise Is Now Clearer
Key Finding 6: Ambiguity Preservation Is Possible But Must Be Protected
Key Finding 8: Predictions / Clarifications Need Human Scoring

sources.role: Aleks' original ABC source philosophy, preserved as source philosophy

purpose: Translate A into B without raw text transfer, memory dumping, identity collapse, or forced denial.

abc_separation.C: future vessel, deferred in this pass

transfer_rules: B stores bounded summaries, source references, rules, tests, and calibration targets.
B never stores full raw conversations as memory.
B treats life-related and symbolic material as possible signal when review/provenance supports it.
B preserves uncertainty labels instead of forcing closure.
B is the rollback layer for future C failure.

category_translations.do_not_transfer: full raw conversations, private unbounded text, or assumed identity continuity
category_translations.b_representation: Anchor registry entries with meanings, ambiguity labels, source references, and calibration notes.
category_translations.review_rule: yes items may become usable B evidence; unsure items remain review-only; no items are excluded from continuity evidence but preserved as provenance.
category_translations.future_c_interface: C may consult this B representation only after B checkpoint review; C does not receive raw A.
